title: Function calling (tools)
summary: Pass Python functions directly as tools — the SDK auto-declares them from type hints and docstrings, runs the round-trip, and returns the final natural-language answer.
notes: Automatic function calling is on by default — disable it with AutomaticFunctionCallingConfig(disable=True) when you want to inspect and execute calls yourself.
Type hints + docstrings are the schema. Keep docstrings imperative ("Get the current temperature ...") — the model uses them to decide when to call.
Multi-step tool plans are common — the response carries the *final* text after all tool turns; per-call tokens roll up in usage_metadata.
